mnemonics: SA Structure: Signal Actions are stored in a sigaction struct (SA) to handle signals more reliably than signal().
keyPoints: The sigaction function provides more control and information about signals than the signal function.
It uses a struct sigaction to specify the handler, signals to block during execution, and flags for behavior control.
It allows for portable and reliable signal handling across different UNIX systems.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check the return value of sigaction for errors, assuming the call always succeeds.
Incorrectly initializing the sigaction struct, leading to undefined behavior (e.g., not zeroing it out first).
